title: per(0) silently limits to zero instead of raising immediately
body: Calling `Model.page(1).per(0)` in page_scope_methods.rb takes the `n.zero?` branch and calls `limit(n)` (i.e. limit(0)) rather than raising right away. The ZeroPerPageOperation error only surfaces later, from total_pages or current_page, which makes the root cause (a stray `.per(0)` call) much harder to trace back from the eventual error.

title: Paginator#relevant_pages recomputes window ranges on every render
body: For a paginated table with a very large total_pages count, Paginator#relevant_pages builds several ranges and unions/sorts them fresh on each call to each_relevant_page. Rendering the same paginator widget twice on a page (e.g. top and bottom of a table) recomputes this from scratch each time. Would memoizing per window_options be reasonable?

title: Precedence between max_paginates_per and Kaminari.config.max_per_page is unclear
body: If a model scope calls `max_paginates_per(50)` but the global initializer also sets `Kaminari.config.max_per_page = 20`, which one actually caps `per()` calls made afterwards? The config.rb docs don't spell out the precedence, and testing it directly gave results that didn't match what I expected from the initializer alone.

title: paginate helper drops existing query params inside a Turbo Frame
body: Using the `paginate` view helper from action_view_extension.rb inside a Turbo Frame, navigating to page 2 loses other query string params (like a search filter) that were present on the original request. Outside of a Turbo Frame the same view keeps them fine, so it looks like the params merging logic doesn't account for Turbo's frame-scoped requests.

title: total_count ignores DISTINCT on joined/grouped relations
body: For a `Model.joins(:tags).page(1)` query where the join can produce duplicate rows, `total_pages` computed via total_count in active_record_relation_methods.rb comes out higher than the actual number of distinct records, giving extra empty pages at the end of pagination.
